subject: Curtain wall attachment at transfer beam
question: Structural drawings indicate transfer beam at floor 5 but curtain wall anchor layout from glazing subcontractor does not coordinate with beam flange width. Request revised embed layout from structural engineer.

subject: MEP coordination at residential corridor floors 10-14
question: Mechanical and electrical runs shown in drawings conflict at corridor ceiling. HVAC duct shown at 18 inches conflicts with electrical conduit bundle. BIM coordination required. Please confirm priority and routing preference.

subject: Parking garage waterproofing spec conflict
question: Spec section 07 14 00 references Plaza deck waterproofing by Tremco but detail on Sheet A8.3 shows traffic-bearing membrane by Sika. Which system applies to Level P1 parking deck? Different primer and termination bar details.

subject: Foundation caisson depth - Bearing stratum elevation
question: Geotech report indicates rock at El -42 ft but encountered rock at El -38 ft on caissons 1-4. Confirm if tip elevation can be adjusted or if full depth is still required. 4-ft adjustment on 68 caissons has significant cost and schedule impact.

subject: Ballroom acoustical ceiling height conflict
question: Hotel program requires 22-ft clear ceiling in main ballroom but structure as designed yields 18-ft 6-in to bottom of beam. Owner requires resolution. Request structural and architectural alternatives.

subject: Dock door threshold detail at freeze-protected slab
question: Specifications call for heated floor slab at dock apron but detail does not show transition to unheated interior slab. Require detail at threshold to prevent cracking from differential thermal movement.

subject: Seismic joint detailing at building expansion joint
question: Structural drawings show 3-inch expansion joint at grid line H but MEP drawings route piping across joint without flexible coupling or expansion loop. Conflict needs resolution prior to MEP installation.

subject: NICU HVAC pressurization and room relationship
question: FGI guidelines require NICU rooms to be positive pressure relative to corridor but mechanical drawings show corridor pressure class as positive. Confirm pressure hierarchy and update mechanical design accordingly.
